---
title: "Fel_normalfĂ¶rdelning"
author: "Emilio Otero"
date: "2026-03-11"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

source("population_model.R")
```

# Simulera fel frĂĄn en normalfĂ¶rdelning

Sex olika fall av $$N(\mu, \sigma)$$:

1. $$\mu = 0, \; \sigma = c$$
2. $$\mu = 0, \; \sigma = \frac{X+1}{10}$$
3. $$\mu = 5 - 0.25 X, \; \sigma = c$$
4. $$\mu = 5 - 0.25 X, \; \sigma = \frac{X+1}{10}$$
5. $$\mu = 5 e^{-0.7675 X}, \; \sigma = c$$
6. $$\mu = 5 e^{-0.7675 X}, \; \sigma = \frac{X+1}{10}$$

## Baseline modell

$$\epsilon  \sim N(0,\sigma^2 )$$
```{r}
baseline_error <- dist_with_error(dist_eigenvector$stable_dist, function(x) 4.5)
plot_overlapping_bars(dist_eigenvector$stable_dist,baseline_error)

```


## Heteroskedastic fel (Ă„ldre bjĂ¶rnar fĂĄr stĂ¶rre fel)

$$\epsilon | X \sim N(0,f(X)^2 )$$

```{r}
baseline_error <- dist_with_error(dist_eigenvector$stable_dist, function(x) 4)
sum(baseline_error)
plot_overlapping_bars(dist_eigenvector$stable_dist,baseline_error)
```

## Truncated error 
$$
\epsilon \,\big|\, X \sim 
\text{TruncatedNormal}\Big(
\mu = 0,\; 
\sigma = f(X),\; 
a = -X,\; 
b = \infty
\Big)
$$

```{r}
baseline_error <- dist_with_error_trunc(dist_eigenvector$stable_dist, function(x) 1)

plot_overlapping_bars(dist_eigenvector$stable_dist,baseline_error)

```

```
